MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction [C.C.P. 418.10]
Off Calendar Sep-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FARRELL LINES INCORPORATED'S MOTION To Quash Service of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction [C.C.P. 418.10]. Appearances: Christine A. Renken, Esq. of Clapper Patti Schweizer & Mason (415) 332-4262, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Andrew I. Port. Esq. and David R. Boyajian, Esq. of Emard Danoff Port Tamulski & Paetzold LLP (415) 227-9455, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to quash granted. Defendant's contacts not substantial, continuous and systematic to demonstrate general jurisdiction. (See DVI, Inc. v. Superior Court (2002) 104 Cal. App. 4th 1080; Cassiar Mining Corp. v. Superior Court (1998) 66 Cal. App. 4th 550.) No demonstration of specific jurisdiction. Plaintiff's cause of action does not arise out of or have a substantial connection with defendant's activities in California. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
